summary.text: Options for
`switchTab`
function.
Navigate and reset the history on route focus.
Forward props to child component and removes the extra
`<View>`
. Useful for custom wrappers.
Event which fires on long press on the tab in the tab bar.
Event which fires on tapping on the tab in the tab bar.
The React Navigation custom navigator.

content.text: ](https://reactnavigation.org/docs/custom-navigators/#usenavigationbuilder) hook from React Navigation for more information.

summary.text: Remove inactive screens.
Override how the
`Screen`
component is rendered.
Forward props to child component and removes the extra
`<View>`
. Useful for custom wrappers.
Options provided to the
`UseTabSlotOptions`
Should the screen be unloaded when inactive.
Index of screen.
Whether the screen is focused.
Whether the screen has been loaded.
Forward props to child component. Useful for custom wrappers.
Name of tab. Required when used within a
`TabList`
Name of tab. When used within a
`TabList`

this sets the name of the tab.
Otherwise, this references the name.
Resets the route when switching to a tab.
Options to provide to the Tab Router.
Wrapper component for
`TabTriggers`
`TabTriggers`
within the
`TabList`
define the tabs.
Root component for the headless tabs.

content.text: ](#usetabswithchildrenoptions) for a hook version of this component.

summary.text: Renders the current tab.

content.text: ](#usetabslot) for a hook version of this component.

summary.text: Creates a trigger to navigate to a tab. When used as child of
`TabList`

, its
functionality slightly changes since the
`href`

prop is required,
and the trigger also defines what routes are present in the
`Tabs`

.

When used outside of
`TabList`
, this component no longer requires an
`href`
Returns a
`ReactElement`
of the current tab.
Hook version of
`Tabs`

. The returned NavigationContent component
should be rendered. Using the hook requires using the
`<TabList />`
and
`<TabTrigger />`

components exported from Expo Router.

The
`useTabsWithTriggers()`
hook can be used for custom components.

content.text: ](#tabs) for the component version of this hook.

summary.text: Create a descriptor object for a route.
Route object for which the descriptor should be created
Whether the descriptor should be a placeholder, e.g. for a route not yet in the state

Returns the navigation prop from a parent navigator based on the ID.
If an ID is provided, the navigation prop from the parent navigator with matching ID (including current) will be returned.
If no ID is provided, the navigation prop from the immediate parent navigator will be returned.
Alternative entries for history.
Index of the currently focused route.
Unique key for the navigation state.
List of valid route names as defined in the screen components.
List of rendered routes.
Whether the navigation state has been rehydrated.

Custom type for the state, whether it's for tab, stack, drawer etc.
During rehydration, the state will be discarded if type doesn't match with router type.
It can also be used to detect the type of the navigator we're dealing with.
Optional ID of a parent navigator.

Update the options for the route.
The options object will be shallow merged with default options object.

Check if dispatching back action will be handled by navigation.
Note that this method doesn't re-render screen when the result changes. So don't use it in
`render`

Dispatch an action or an update function to the router.
The update function will receive the current state,
Action object or update function.
Additional data for the action
Key of the route which dispatched this action.
Key of the navigator which should handle this action.
Type of the action (e.g.
`NAVIGATE`
Returns the name of the navigator specified in the
`name`

prop.
If no name is specified, returns
`undefined`

Returns the navigation helpers from a parent navigator based on the ID.
If an ID is provided, the navigation helper from the parent navigator with matching ID (including current) will be returned.
If no ID is provided, the navigation helper from the immediate parent navigator will be returned.
Optional ID of a parent navigator.

Returns the navigator's state.
Note that this method doesn't re-render screen when the result changes. So don't use it in
`render`
Go back to the previous route in history.
Check if the screen is focused. The method returns
`true`
if focused,
`false`

otherwise.
Note that this method doesn't re-render screen when the focus changes. So don't use it in
`render`

.
To get notified of focus changes, use
`addListener('focus', cb)`
and
`addListener('blur', cb)`

.
To conditionally render content based on focus state, use the
`useIsFocused`
hook.
